references.text: Beech, E, M Barstow & M Rivers. 2017. The red list of Theaceae. Botanic Gardens Conservation International, UK
Bloembergen, S.1952. A critical study in the complex-polymorphous genus Schima (Theaceae). Reinwardtia 2: 133-183
Keng, H. 1978. Theaceae. Tree Flora of Malaya 3: 275-296.
Keng, H. 1980. The genus Pyrenaria (Theaceae) in Malesia. Gardens’ Bulletin Singapore 33: 264-289
Keng, H. 1984. The genus Gordonia (Theaceae) in Malesia. Gardens’ Bulletin Singapore 37: 1-47
Li, R, J-B Yang, S-X Yang & D-Z Li. 2011. Phylogeny and taxonomy of the Pyrenaria complex (Theaceae) based on nuclear ribosomal ITS sequences. Nordic Journal of Botany 29: 780-787
Orel, G, PG Wilson, AS Curry & HT Luu. 2013. Two new species of Polyspora (Theaceae) from Vietnam and new combinations for some Asian species. Willdenowia 43: 301-308
Prince LM & CR Parks. 2001. Phylogenetic relationships of Theaceae inferred from chloroplast DNA sequence data. American Journal of Botany 88: 2309-2320

Yang SX, JB Yang, LG Lei, DZ Li, H Yoshino & T Ikeda. 2004. Reassessing the relationships between Gordonia and Polyspora (Theaceae) based on the combined analyses of molecular data from the nuclear, plastid and mitochondrial genomes. Plant Systematics and Evolution 248: 45-55

Zhao, D-W, TR Hodkinson & JAN Parnell. 2022. Phylogenetics of global Camellia (Theaceae) based on three nuclear regions and its implications for systematics and evolutionary history. Journal of Systematics and Evolution 61: 356-368.
Zhao, D-W. 2022. Typification of six names in Camellia (Theaceae). PhytoKeys 201: 15-22

species.citation: (Blume) Seem. , Trans. Linn. Soc. London, 1st Ser. 22 (1859) 345 ; Coritico et al., PJS 151 (2022) 2441; Coritico et al., J. Trop. Biol. & Cons. 19 (2022) 150; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific biodiv. 17 (2024) 13; ≡ Calpandria lanceolata Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1825) 179; ≡ Thea lanceolata (Blume) Pierre , Fl. Forest. Cochinch. 8 (1887) t.119 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 69. = Salceda montana Blanco , Fl. Filip., 2nd Ed. (1845) 374 ; Zhao, Phytokeys 193 (2022) 3; ≡ Thea montana (Blanco) Merr. , Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 27 (1905) 21; ≡ Camellia montana (Blanco) Hung T.Chang & S.X.Ren , Acta Sci. Nat. Univ. Sunyatseni 30 (1991) 68; Type: BS 34071 Ramos & Edaño (K, neo; BM, P. US, isoneo), Luzon: Bulacan, Angat, Feb-1919. = Camellia furfuracea subsp. megacarpa (Elmer) T.L.Ming , Acta Bot. Yunnan. 21 (1999) 155; ≡ Camellia megacarpa (Elmer) Cohen-Stuart , Meded. Proefstat. Thee 11 (1916) 68 ; Chang & Ren, Acta Sci. Nat. Univ. Sunyatseni 30 (1991) 69; ≡ Thea megacarpa Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1842 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 70; Zhao, PhytoKeys 201 (2022) 20; Type: Elmer 12822 (E, lecto; A, BM, E, G, K, MO, NY, P, U, US, isolecto), Palawan: Puerto Princesa, Mt Pulgar), Mar-1911. = Thea lanceolata var. microphylla Merr. , EPFP 3 (1923) 69; Type: FB 22401 Alvarez, Luzon: Nueva Ecija. = Thea lanceolata var. stenophylla Merr. , EPFP 3 (1923) 70 ; Zhao, PhytoKeys 201 (2022) 18; ≡ Camellia stenophylla (Merr.) Kobuski , Brittonia 4 (1941) 115; Type: BS 33005 Ramos (US, lecto; K, isolecto), Luzon: Ilocos Norte, Bangui to Claveria, Aug-1918.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Philippines, Sulawesi, Thailand to Sumatra. BASILAN, GUIMARAS, HOMONHON, LUZON: Apayao, Aurora, Bataan, Batangas, Benguet, Cagayan, Camarines Sur, Cavite, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Ecija, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Tarlac, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Davao del Sur, Lanao, Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, POLILLO, SAMAR.
species.notes: In Luzon from Cagayan to Sorsogon. Primary forests, 700-2000m; often very common. Beech et al. (2017) consider Camellia montana distinct from Camellia lanceolata.

species.citation: (Merr.) Hung T.Chang & S.X.Ren , Acta Sci. Nat. Univ. Sunyatseni 30 (1991) 69; ≡ Thea lanceolata var. mollis Merr. , EPFP 3 (1923) 69; Type: BS 34071 Ramos & Edaño, Luzon: Bulacan, Angat, Feb-1919.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Bulacan, Cagayan, Quezon, Rizal, MINDORO.

species.citation: Hung T.Chang & S.X.Ren , Acta Sci. Nat. Univ. Sunyatseni 30 (1991) 68; Type: BS 39600 Ramos (US, holo; A, iso), Mindoro: Paluan, Apr-1921.

species.citation: (S.Vidal) Orel, Peter G.Wilson, Curry & Luu , Willdenowia 43 (2013) 306; ≡ Gordonia luzonica S.Vidal , Revis. Pl. Vasc. Filip. (1886) 57 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 70; Keng, Gard. Bull. Sing. 37 (1984) 21; Type: Vidal 63 (MA), Luzon: Tayabas, Monte Banahao. = Gordonia benguetica Burkill , PJS 15 (1919) 478 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 70; Type: FB 5083 Curran (SING, iso), Luzon: Benguet, Baguio, Aug-1906. = Gordonia fragrans Merr. , PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 95. = Gordonia subclavata Burkill , PJS 15 (1919) 478 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 71. = Gordonia welborni Elmer , LPB 2 (1908) 500; Type: Elmer 9584 (L, NY, US, iso), Negros: Negros Oriental, Cuernos Mtns, in forest, elevation 4250ft (1295m), Mar-1908.
species.distribution: New Guinea, Philippines. BILIRAN, CEBU (photos), DINAGAT (photos), LUZON: Abra, Apayao (photos), Bataan, Benguet, Camarines Sur, Ifugao, La Union, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Davao Oriental, Davao del Sur, Lanao, Misamis Occidental, Misamis Oriental (photos), Surigao, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY.
species.notes: Primary forests, stream depressions and exposed ridges. 600-1300m.

species.citation: (Burkill) Orel, Peter G.Wilson, Curry & Luu , Willdenowia 43 (2013) 308; ≡ Gordonia polisana Burkill , PJS 15 (1919) 478 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 71; Type: FB 18384 Alvarez (US), Luzon: Ifugao, Mt Polis, Jan-1909.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Benguet, Ifugao, Nueva Vizcaya.

species.citation: (Melch.) Orel, Peter G.Wilson, Curry & Luu , Willdenowia 43 (2013) 308; ≡ Gordonia sablayana Melch. , Nat. Pflanzenfam. (Engler & Prantl), 2nd Ed. 21 (1925) 137; Type: FB 9757 Merritt (K), Mindoro: Feb/Apr-1908.

species.citation: (DC.) Korth. , Verh. Nat. Gesch. Ned. Bezitt., Bot. (1842) 143; ≡ Gordonia wallichii DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 1 (1824) 528. ssp. crenata (Korth.) Bloemb., Reinwardtia 2 (1952) 172; ≡ Schima crenata Korth., Verh. Nat. Gesch. Ned. Bezitt., Bot. (1842) 143. Distribution: Bangladesh, Bhutan, China, India, Laos, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sumatra, Thailand, Tibet, Vietnam. PALAWAN. Native. var. pulgarensis (Elmer) Bloemb., Reinwardtia 2 (1952) 175; ≡ Schima pulgarensis Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1843; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 71; Type: Elmer 13191. ≠ Schima noronhae auct. non Reinw. ex Blume; Merr. & Rolfe, PJS 3 c (1908) Bot. 113. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. PALAWAN. Native.
